finding: Needs Ghostscript + Tesseract binaries; dependency-chain risk must be patched/maintained.
cve_watch: Indirect risk via Ghostscript/Tesseract chain; track dependency advisories.
recommendation: Approve conditionally with patch cadence + locked versions.

finding: Permissive license; mature OCR engine; keep current because OCR surfaces parser attack input.
cve_watch: Watch upstream + distro CVEs; avoid stale binaries.
recommendation: Approve from official GitHub or vetted distro channel only.

finding: Frequent PDF parser CVEs; GPL may trigger policy/legal review.
recommendation: Approve only with strict update SLA and legal sign-off; or use approved alternative PDF render path.

finding: Image parser historically receives memory safety CVEs; current pin should stay patched.
cve_watch: Example recent issue CVE-2026-25990 fixed in 12.1.1.
recommendation: Keep pinned to latest fixed version; rescan quarterly.

finding: Recent XSS advisories in user-supplied diagram rendering contexts.
recommendation: Approve only if pinned to patched versions and rendered in sandboxed mode.

finding: Enterprise-common diagram tool; verify license compatibility statement.
cve_watch: Track Graphviz CVEs if renderer is exposed.
recommendation: Good fallback for deterministic diagram output.

finding: Low controversy package for PPT generation/editing.
cve_watch: No major obvious red flags in quick pass.

finding: Useful for Office/Visio COM automation; increases endpoint automation risk surface.
cve_watch: No major obvious red flags in quick pass; treat as privileged automation.
recommendation: Approve only if native Visio/Office automation is mandatory.

finding: Primary risk is data governance/policy, not package CVEs.
recommendation: Approve with endpoint allowlist + logging + DLP.

finding: Use KEV as rejection/conditional gate for actively exploited vulnerabilities.
cve_watch: Reject or conditionally approve unresolved KEV exposure.
recommendation: Make KEV check mandatory in software approval workflow.
